subject: Computer Networking
title: Signal and Privacy-First Design
description: A micro-lesson that makes Signal and Privacy-First Design usable.

hook.text: Signal collects almost nothing about you. It's the gold standard for private messaging.

buildup.text: Signal Protocol uses double-ratchet encryption. Keys rotate with every message for forward secrecy.

discovery.text: Signal stores only your phone number and last connection date. No message content, no contacts, no metadata.

twist.text: When subpoenaed, Signal can only hand over two data points: the phone number and last connection timestamp.

climax.text: Sealed sender hides who sent a message even from Signal's servers. Metadata protection taken to the extreme.

punchline.text: Signal proves privacy and usability can coexist. No compromise.

quiz.question: What does Signal's double-ratchet encryption provide?
options: Forward secrecy by rotating keys with every message
Faster message delivery than other apps
